description:
Goal
Make compiler2 native lowering birth and transport reusable-cons-cell physical facts through compiler-generated control flow.

Why
`BackendStep::SplitList` currently lowers to `Prim::ListHead(source)` and `Prim::ListTail(source)` and then drops the physical relationship between `source` and `head`. Old-world solved the analogous problem by capturing hidden owned-cons sources in continuations. Compiler2 should implement the same principle in its own native/artifact seams, not import old-world CPS/planner structure.

Algorithm
1. At `src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs` `BackendStep::SplitList { source, head, tail }`:
   - lower `ListHead(source)` and bind semantic `head` as today;
   - lower `ListTail(source)` and bind semantic `tail` as today;
   - record a reusable-cons capability: semantic head var -> physical source cons var.
2. Maintain a native-lowering environment mapping semantic IR vars to optional physical source-cons vars.
3. When creating/calling compiler-generated entries/continuations/helpers:
   - inspect the semantic captures already required by the entry;
   - for each captured semantic value that has a reusable-cons source in the caller, append the source cons as an extra hidden physical capture argument;
   - append physical params after all semantic params in `entry_signature`;
   - mark those params as physical entry params in `FnIr`;
   - on entry, restore the semantic value -> source cons capability using the new IR builder API.
4. Keep physical captures process-local. Do not transport reusable sender heap refs through send/receive/message state, provider boundaries, or heap-escaped closures unless runtime semantics explicitly copy/alias them first.

Seams to inspect/update
- `src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs`: `entry_signature`, `entry_capture_args`, `bind_entry_input`, `delivered_args_for_abi`, branches/case arm transitions, miss entries, resume deliveries.
- `src/compiler2/artifact.rs`: only add artifact fields if native lowering cannot derive the physical capture set from existing entry/capture metadata. Prefer deriving over duplicating.
- `src/fz_ir/mod.rs`: use the new reusable-cons builder API.

Invariants
- Physical capture params are invisible to semantic identity and type dispatch.
- Physical capture ordering is deterministic and appended after semantic captures.
- If a continuation captures the head but not the source cons, the ticket is wrong; the source must travel with the semantic value that will consume the credit.
- If a semantic value is no longer live/captured, its physical source must not be kept alive just because it once existed.

Tests
- Add a focused compiler2 native lowering test with a recursive or branchy list reconstruction where the construction occurs in a generated continuation, not the same lexical block as `SplitList`.
- Assert the resulting `FnIr` has physical entry params and reusable-cons capabilities on the continuation/helper, while semantic keys ignore the physical params.
- Run the targeted `fixture_matrix` tests that currently pin physical params/reuse behavior.

Acceptance
Reusable-cons facts survive the compiler2-native control-flow boundaries that currently lose them, without adding any semantic fact kind or source-visible parameter.
title: Compiler2 native lowering: birth and transport reusable-cons facts through physical captures
